rule.problem: Combined indicators (LAT + RPKT + TO) suggest a multi-factor network issue affecting stability and performance.
diagnostics: Validate interface errors, drops, and utilization on impacted hops
Verify routing symmetry and firewall/NAT session behavior for affected flows
Correlate timeframe of LAT + RPKT + TO indicators with device CPU/memory and queue depth
fix: 1. Offload or upgrade overloaded devices urgently
4. Implement traffic bypass for trusted flows

rule.problem: Packets are delayed beyond TCP Retransmission Timeout (RTO) values (typically 200ms-3s depending on configuration). This forces TCP to assume packet loss and trigger expensive retransmission timeouts. Can be caused by extreme congestion, device CPU overload freezing packet forwarding, or processing delays in security appliances (firewall deep packet inspection, IPS scanning).
diagnostics: Check maximum queue depths and peak latency values
Monitor firewall/IPS processing delays and session table utilization
Review device CPU spikes correlating with timeout periods
Check for routing loops or suboptimal path selection (traceroute analysis)
Examine security appliance logs for dropped/delayed sessions
Monitor application server backend database query times
Check for DNS resolution delays
fix: 1. Offload firewall/IPS to more powerful hardware or distribute load across multiple devices
2. Tune firewall session timeout values appropriately for applications
3. Optimize security policy rules (move frequently hit rules to top, remove unnecessary deep inspection)
4. Increase device resources or optimize control plane protection
5. Implement bypass mechanisms for trusted internal traffic
6. Adjust TCP RTO parameters on servers if appropriate for environment (careful tuning required)
7. Fix routing issues to prevent suboptimal paths (adjust metrics, fix routing protocols)
8. Optimize slow database queries causing application-layer delays
9. Enable hardware acceleration for crypto/inspection functions

rule.problem: Internal path latency is elevated due to internal congestion, queueing, or device processing delay.
diagnostics: Check core/distribution link utilization and queue depth
Check switch/router/firewall CPU and forwarding latency
Validate interface health and duplex/speed consistency
fix: 1. Apply QoS priority for latency-sensitive traffic
2. Rebalance traffic away from congested links
3. Fix interface errors and duplex mismatch

rule.problem: Network congestion or buffer overflow at intermediate hops. Queue buildup increases latency while full buffers drop packets requiring retransmission. May also indicate interface speed mismatch or oversubscribed uplinks.
diagnostics: Check switch/router interface statistics (show interface counters, queue depth)
Monitor bandwidth utilization on core/distribution layer switches (SNMP, NetFlow)
Review QoS policies and queue configurations
Check for jumbo frame mismatches between segments
Examine buffer allocation on switches handling this traffic
Look for egress queue drops in interface statistics
fix: 1. Identify congested links via SNMP monitoring (>80% utilization sustained)
2. Implement or adjust QoS policies to prioritize critical traffic
3. Upgrade link capacity on oversubscribed segments
4. Enable flow control (IEEE 802.3x) if appropriate for environment
6. Consider traffic shaping to smooth bursts
7. Deploy LACP link aggregation for additional bandwidth
8. Segment traffic using VLANs to isolate heavy consumers

diagnostics: Validate interface errors, drops, and utilization on impacted hops
Verify routing symmetry and firewall/NAT session behavior for affected flows
Correlate timeframe of LAT + RPKT + R% + PQF indicators with device CPU/memory and queue depth

Verify routing symmetry and firewall/NAT session behavior for affected flows
Correlate timeframe of LAT + RPKT + R% indicators with device CPU/memory and queue depth
fix: 1. Emergency capacity expansion on congested segments
3. Enable WRED/ECN to reduce synchronized TCP backoff
4. Increase buffer allocations on core switches
5. Deploy traffic shaping to smooth bursts
8. Implement admission control for non-critical traffic

rule.problem: Internal session timeouts indicate severe delay/loss or processing bottlenecks.
diagnostics: Check firewall/IPS processing delay
Check queue peak latency and drops
Verify route stability and absence of loops
fix: 2. Repair hotspot links and queuing saturation
3. Stabilize routing and device resource usage
